timeline.label: FY2025 results: EPS HK$14.05 and full-year dividend HK$12.52 per share (+10% YoY)
Q1 2026 revenue HK$8.2B (+20% vs Q1 2025) confirms strong run-rate after FY2025 record
FY2024 record year: revenue HK$22.4B (+9%), profit HK$13.1B (+10%), ADT HK$131.8B (+26%)
Completed acquisition of London Metal Exchange, adding global commodities clearing to franchise
Stock Connect launch with Shanghai expanded cross-border equity flow through Hong Kong
Shenzhen Stock Connect opened, deepening mainland-Hong Kong equity link
Bond Connect southbound trading launched, extending fixed-income access channel
Nicolas Aguzin appointed Group CEO, succeeding Charles Li after long tenure

industry.structure: HKEX is the dominant listing and trading gateway between international capital and Chinese issuers, operating cash equities, derivatives, LME commodities, and central clearing with high consolidated margins (~74% EBITDA).
industry.share_shift: Mainland exchanges (Shanghai, Shenzhen) grow domestic listings, but Connect programs keep Hong Kong as the preferred offshore channel for China exposure; global exchange peers (CME, ICE, LSEG) compete on derivatives and data.
industry.trend: Record FY2024–FY2025 volumes reflect cyclical boom in China-linked trading; regulatory scrutiny of Hong Kong's financial-center role and LME market-structure reforms remain ongoing watch items.
